commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This needs human maintainer review because the remaining blocker combines a concrete v1 compatibility repair with an intentional product-security coverage reduction.; Needs attention: The diff intentionally retires dependency-registry existence checks, which is a security-coverage reduction that needs explicit maintainer acceptance before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Keep the v1 dependencyRegistry signal null... commands.review_summary:
The PR removes dependency registry scan scheduling and scanner modules, scrubs legacy dependency-registry findings from public outputs, adds an admin cleanup action, and updates docs/specs/tests to stop claiming dependency-registry coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blocker: current main documents and returns a nullable `dependencyRegistry` signal, while the PR head removes the key from the v1 response builders. I verified this by source inspection rather than a live API run.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs human maintainer review because the remaining blocker combines a concrete v1 compatibility repair with an intentional product-security coverage reduction.
